behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (request builder -> API client payload), not just isolated parsing logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network required; a captured in-process client stub is sufficient. LLM-DEP: does not depend on model judgment, only payload composition. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is about 'request sent ... includes ... labels' (request wiring), not live model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API client that records outbound requests; invoke classification with a known taxonomy and assert every call includes the full taxonomy list in the payload.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: specific failure is local normalization/validation logic (taxonomy membership check), no required cross-module state propagation. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none; use fixed raw label input. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on mapping an out-of-taxonomy value to 'unknown', which is a single-function rule.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/mapping function with taxonomy {legal, technical, financial, other} and raw label 'medical'; assert result is exactly 'unknown'.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: deterministic transformation property of one local step. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none because input is identical fixed raw response. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly scopes to 'normalization step' determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly (or property-test) with the same raw API response fixture and taxonomy; assert output label is identical across runs.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: behavior explicitly requires 'repeated live API calls' and consistency tied to provider response changes; a stub cannot expose real model/provider variability. REAL-INFRA exists, but the real failure boundary is model/API behavior under repeated calls, so Agentic is the minimum. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration/System because behavior text requires live provider behavior, not only transport/retry mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls against the provider for the same document+taxonomy, capture raw provider responses and normalized labels, and assert label changes only when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at boundary between token-budget policy, request builder, and dispatch gate (e.g., reject must prevent send). REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS required; in-process fake dispatcher can prove pre-dispatch enforcement. LLM-DEP: none; token budgeting logic can be tested deterministically. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Unit because behavior includes 'before dispatch' side effect across modules, not just token-count arithmetic.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a low token budget, wire module to a fake dispatcher, submit oversized input, and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) plus that outbound dispatch reflects policy (truncated payload or no send).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real provider token accounting from an AI API call; mock usage reports can trivially pass and hide real tokenizer/model-accounting mismatches. REAL-INFRA is involved, but the decisive boundary is real model/provider behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because fake responses cannot validate true provider-reported usage; rejected pure System because the assertion targets model/provider accounting semantics, not just HTTP mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative real documents to the live provider via the module, read provider-reported token usage metadata, and assert usage <= configured budget per call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: retry correctness is module orchestration (error handling + backoff + second attempt) across components. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process stub returning rate-limit signal then success; real network not required for this failure mode. LLM-DEP: none (infrastructure behavior around API call). REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text targets retry control flow on 'rate too high', not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client that returns rate-limit error on first call and success on second; assert module waits/backs off, retries, and returns successful label instead of propagating the initial error.
